vocab.meaning: tràn đầy năng lượng và hăng hái
vocab.example: As a kid, I was always full of beans, so my parents would often take me for long walks in the park to burn off some steam.

vocab.word: know somewhere like the back of one's hand
vocab.ipa: /nəʊ ˈsʌmweə(r) laɪk ðə bæk əv wʌnz hænd/
vocab.meaning: biết rõ nơi nào đó như lòng bàn tay
vocab.example: I used to wander around my neighborhood so much that I grew to know every little alleyway like the back of my hand.

vocab.meaning: đi bộ trên những con đường quen thuộc (nghĩa rộng: dạo bước)
vocab.example: I remember spending hours treading the boards of the local boardwalk with my siblings during our summer holidays.

vocab.meaning: đến những nơi hẻo lánh/ít người qua lại
vocab.example: Even as a young boy, I loved going for walks that took me off the beaten track to discover hidden spots in the woods.
Whenever I went out for a stroll in the countryside, I would often lose track of time because I was so captivated by the scenery.

vocab.meaning: hòa mình/gần gũi với thiên nhiên
vocab.example: Going for walks in the fields made me feel at one with nature, a feeling I’ve cherished since I was a small child.

vocab.meaning: đi đường vòng để ngắm cảnh đẹp
vocab.example: My father always insisted on taking the scenic route when we walked to the grocery store, just so we could enjoy the gardens.

vocab.meaning: một luồng gió mới/sự thay đổi dễ chịu (nghĩa đen: không khí trong lành)
vocab.example: After a morning of studying, going outside for a walk was a real breath of fresh air for me as a youngster.

vocab.meaning: tăng tốc/đi nhanh hơn (hoặc nỗ lực hơn)
vocab.example: When the sun started to set, my mother would tell us to step it up so we could reach home before dark.

vocab.word: on the tip of one's toes
vocab.meaning: đi nhón chân (hào hứng hoặc thận trọng)
vocab.example: I remember walking on the tip of my toes with excitement every time we approached the local candy shop.
